key_insight: SciPhyRL turns portfolio optimization into a data-driven pathwise HJB problem and solves it offline with a PINN to learn cost-aware, distribution-aware trading policies.
engineering_relevance: The framework offers institutional allocators a practical way to convert signals into executable multi-period decisions while explicitly accounting for market impact and turnover.
method: Reformulate continuous-time portfolio control in an extended state space that includes cumulative costs.
Project the HJB equation onto observed trajectories and solve it directly from historical data with a PINN in one offline pass.
Use a discrete target-holding action and a quadratic price-impact model to make short-horizon execution realistic.
evidence_signal: On a 14-asset ETF universe with an engineered oracle signal, the learned Gibbs policy improves out-of-sample Sharpe ratio versus static and myopic baselines while controlling volatility and turnover.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of the offline signal and the realism of the impact model, so misspecification could reduce live-trading robustness.
paper_title: SciPhy Reinforcement Learning for Portfolio Optimization
